fault_hypothesis: Line 67 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`now > adjusted_ttl`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= adjusted_ttl`), so when `now` exactly equals the adjusted TTL the entry is incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

constraints.intent: When now equals adjusted_ttl the condition must treat the entry as expired; the strict > comparison fails this at the boundary.
Sanity check that adjusted_ttl__1 is defined and the boundary case (now == adjusted_ttl__1) is handled by the subsequent comparison using >=.
Flag the exact-boundary case before the comparison so the tool can detect that now == adjusted_ttl__1 leads to incorrect non-expiry.
After the else branch (entry not expired), now must be strictly less than adjusted_ttl; equality should have triggered expiry.
